# split_h_2p_demo — Audit Protocol audit-1

**Branch/commit audited:** `claude/persp-c-horiz-review-ntqlca` @ `088d0d1` (15 files: `split_h_2p_demo` rail, `tools/gen_pose_tables.py`, tests, guide/roadmap/paper-cuts/provenance).
**Role:** independent AUDIT-1 — I did not build this increment. All evidence below regenerated by me from a fresh materialization (`dryrun_split.sh /tmp/kit_audit1`), all measurements taken on the cycle-accurate emulator via MesenRunner; nothing in `/home/user/SuperForge` was modified (`git status` clean, HEAD = 088d0d1).
**Method:** fresh materialize → build default + 4 variants → run the 16 new tests + 56-test split-family regression → all four gates → end-to-end read of `main.asm` (width, allocator interplay, VBlank bounding, latch model) → independent emulator probes (cadence, seam offset, origin math on the framebuffer, retarget pointer flip, latch tear, fold control) → adversarial cadence-gate discrimination via deliberately-burdened builds → independent reimplementation of the pose-table math → asset regeneration byte-compare → visual inspection of my own renders.

## VERDICT: CLEAN (ship)

Every claim in the increment reproduced from scratch on the emulator, including the headline: the loop closes every frame (20 stepped frames, loop counter and NMI counter +1/+1 in lockstep), and I demonstrated the cadence gate genuinely discriminates — a build burdened to ~1.4 frames of work fails the exact gate procedure at step 1 with the predicted 30 Hz quantization (+6 loop / +12 NMI), while a ~0.9-frame burden passes. The origin math renders correctly (stripe edges and checker-row positions match the pure-subtraction + hyperbolic-ramp predictions to ≤1 pixel/row on the framebuffer, in both bands), the seam is at exactly scanline 112 with the +7 screenshot offset empirically confirmed, retarget flips exactly the two ROM pointers I located independently in the image, the latch control tears (worst jitter 6.53 vs deterministic clean baseline 1.58) under a confound-free frozen-vs-frozen comparison, the tooling is byte-identical to an independent reimplementation of its documented math, committed assets regenerate byte-identically, and all four gates (including provenance) are clean. The only notes are info-severity; none require remediation before ship.

## Findings

1. **[info] `gen_pose_tables.py` docstring claims a "fit assertion warns" for the 128-angle set; no such assertion exists.** `tools/gen_pose_tables.py:24` — the code emits an informational `print` ("needs 2 LoROM banks"), not an assertion/warning. The budget is instead enforced in `tests/test_gen_pose_tables.py:74` (asserts 2 banks for 128, 1 for 32/64). Doc nit only.
2. **[info] "Same value the HDMA delivers" in the latch model is exact only for band 1 — which is the only band measured.** `main.asm:44-48, 420-436`: the spin writes band-1's HOFS (384) all frame; during band-2 display the register's HDMA-delivered value is 640, so band 2 additionally suffers a value stomp. The test's jitter window is scanlines 20–100 (band 1 only, `test_split_h_2p_demo.py:306-311`), so the pure-latch-interleave attribution *as tested* is sound; the comment would be more precise saying "same value as band-1's channel value".
3. **[info] The latch tear is phase-intermittent — a potential (low) flake source.** In my 6-grab window only 2 grabs caught the tear (per-sample jitter: 6.53, 1.58, 1.58, 6.53, 1.58, 1.58; clean = 1.58 exactly). Worst-of-6 passed comfortably here and in the suite run, but a window landing all-clean would fail spuriously. Consider widening the sample window if it ever flakes. `test_split_h_2p_demo.py:309-313`.
4. **[info] `test_m1` captures the reference screenshot twice under one tag.** `test_split_h_2p_demo.py:245-246` (and 255-256): the two `_grab(runner, "mot_a")` calls are separate captures, so the band-1 and band-2 reference signatures come from different frames and the second file overwrites the first. Harmless for the assertion (each signature is self-consistent against the later grab); hygiene only.
5. **[info] The committed latch variant's loop itself overruns the frame** — measured +10 loop / +20 NMI over 20 stepped frames. Inherent to the 2000-iteration mid-display spin and carries no 60 fps claim (frozen scene, frozen comparison), but note it is also an in-tree existence proof that the cadence metric detects a 2-frame loop. Related: the "~40 CPU stores/frame" figure in `main.asm:51`/guide is conservative — I count ~13 stores/~25 instructions (~200 CPU cycles ≈ 0.5 % of a frame) in the default loop body; the claim errs in the safe direction and the cadence gate measures the reality.

**Verified with no findings (highlights of the adversarial pass):**
- **Width discipline:** every boundary in `main.asm` annotated (`.a8/.a16/.i16`); NMI saves/restores via `php/rep/pha … pla/plp` correctly balanced; `@bind_done`, `@walk/@set/@next`, `@latch_spin` all single-width-reachable; `width-check` clean with no baseline change in the increment.
- **`split_mask`/`set_indirect_banks`:** walked the bit/base loop by hand — covers CH0..CH7 exactly, terminates at $0100; lowest-bit extraction (`eor #$FFFF`/`inc`/`and`) correct; DASB (`$43x7`) written with DB=$00 (set by `sf_coldstart`, preserved by `hdma_alloc_init`/`hdma_request`/`hdma_bind_direct` — verified in `engine/hdma_alloc.asm`); pose bank = the code bank, matching the 16-bit indirect pointers. Fail-soft carry convention matches `hdma_request` (C=0 success, verified at `hdma_alloc.asm:284`).
- **VBlank bounding of `stamp_origins`:** loop body after `wai` ≈ 200 CPU cycles (counter 14 + motion 36 + stamp ≈ 146 + jmp 3) plus ~60-cycle NMI ≈ 1.2 scanlines of the ~38-scanline VBlank — two orders of magnitude of headroom; corroborated empirically by 10-stepped-frame byte-stability and the deterministic clean-jitter baseline.
- **Index/origin table hardware model:** repeat-mode count 112 ≤ 127; 3-byte indirect entries + terminator = 7 B; origin non-repeat `[112,…][1,…][0]` fires at lines 0/112 and holds; registers hold band-2 values through line 223; all runtime table writes VBlank-only, ahead of the next frame's init fetch.
- **Test vacuity:** a mis-modeled screenshot offset would *fail* the seam test (not silently pass); T1's ramp assertion cannot pass on a held matrix (constant run length); C1 has the SAME_ORIGIN flip control (verified: band-2 red 255 → 0); S1's metric non-vacuity is delegated to R1 through the same capture path (verified firing: 398 changed px); the cadence gate cannot pass on a free-running loop (loop counter would over-advance) or an overrunning one (demonstrated below).

Independent emulator probes (my own script, WRAM + framebuffer):

```
[PASS] A  cadence default, 20 stepped frames: G_FRAMES/E010 lockstep, final (20,20)
[INFO] A2 latch-build cadence over 20 frames: G_FRAMES +10, NMI +20   (2-frame loop, detected by the metric)
[INFO] screenshot size = (256, 239)
[PASS] C  seam red step at image y=119  (= scanline 112 + the modeled +7 offset)
[PASS] C  stripe edges band1-far at x=[43,214]; band2-far at x=[43,214]  (predicted 128±256/(2·1.5) → HOFS=posx−128 exact)
[PASS] C  band-1 vertical checker flips at scanlines [2,13,27,45,71] vs S(k)=K/(k+k0) prediction [3,13,27,46,72] (≤1 row)
[PASS] C  band-2 flips identical at +112 (band-local ramp restart confirmed)
[PASS] D  WRAM tables == formulas: M7X/Y=(512,512),(768,512); HOFS/VOFS=(384,400),(640,288) = pos−128 / pos−112/−224
[PASS] E  stepped pan rates: ΔPOS1Y=+5, ΔPOS2Y=+10 over 5 frames (+1/+2 px/frame)
[PASS] E2 rendered flip rows [8,20,36,59,92] match prediction for WRAM-read posy=590 (bracket [590,591], max err 1 row)
[PASS] F  retarget pointer flip: IDX_AB+4 $8504→$8884, IDX_CD+4 $86C4→$8A44 == ROM addresses of poses1/pose_rot45 blobs
[PASS] F  band1 diff px=0 (byte-stable), band2 diff px=398 (re-rendered); post render = 45° diamond checker, seam intact
[PASS] G  latch tear frozen-vs-frozen: clean jitter=1.58 (deterministic), latch worst=6.53 (per-sample: 6.53,1.58,1.58,6.53,1.58,1.58)
[PASS] H  SAME_ORIGIN fold: band-2 red 0 (vs 255 in freeze)
```

Cadence-gate discrimination (my burdened builds: `dey/bne` burn injected after `stamp_origins`, FREEZE=1, assets untouched; exact gate procedure):

```
burn_7000  (~0.9 frame total loop): final +12 loop / +12 NMI over 12 steps; gate PASSES (lockstep)
burn_14000 (~1.4 frame total loop): final  +6 loop / +12 NMI over 12 steps; gate FAILS at step 1 (G+0, NMI+1)
```

Tooling (independent reimplementation + regeneration):

```
ramp endpoints: 384, 160 (= 1.5·256, 0.625·256); monotone non-increasing: True
angles=32: 14336 B → 1 bank; angles=64: 28672 B → 1 bank (exact fit); angles=128: 57344 B → 2 banks
64-set == my independent math (all 64 poses, byte-identical): True
committed assets == regenerated: checker_map.bin (32768 B), poses1_ab/cd.bin, pose_rot45_ab/cd.bin — all True
pose_rot45_ab == 64-set slice at index 8: True; line-0 A=B=272 (= round(384·cos45°))
```

Renders (mine, from my binaries, in `/tmp/audit1_shots/`): `freeze.png` — two perspective checker floors, cool band over warm band, clean single-line seam; `retarget_post.png` — band 1 unchanged, band 2 a 45°-rotated diamond checker, seam intact; `sameorigin.png` — both bands cool (warm only at the far-row corners, the documented wide-FOV mixing); `latch_0.png` — floor shredded into horizontally displaced strips (the latch-interleave tear).
